frameworks.name: sample, simulate, update (SSUP)
frameworks.one_line: agent samples actions from priors, mentally simulates outcomes, updates beliefs from observations

frameworks.name: mental simulation / intuitive physics
frameworks.one_line: imagined outcomes of hypothesized actions support flexible physical problem solving

frameworks.name: action priors with Bayesian updating
frameworks.one_line: rich priors over actions are revised via observed effects to focus search

frameworks.name: creative tool use
frameworks.one_line: distinctive human capacity to use objects in novel ways to achieve goals

core_question: How do people solve novel physical puzzles with creative tool use in only a handful of attempts?
key_assumptions: people can imagine the effects of hypothesized actions before acting
action search is guided by rich priors over plausible actions
priors are updated from observed outcomes during the task
manipulations: puzzle configuration across 30 levels of the Virtual Tools game
choice of tool object available for placement
key_findings: humans solve a wide range of challenging physical puzzles within a handful of attempts
the SSUP model captures human performance across all 30 puzzle levels
interpretations: flexibility of physical problem solving rests on imagining hypothesized action effects
efficiency of search arises from rich action priors updated by observations
